_meta.description: Table 3: Imaging Criteria Used in the Extended Window Thrombolysis Trials

trials.criteria: DWI/FLAIR mismatch: presence of an abnormal signal on DWI and no visible signal change on FLAIR in the region of the acute stroke
trials.additional_criteria: DWI lesion smaller than one-third of the MCA territory

trials.criteria: DWI/FLAIR mismatch: presence of an abnormal signal on DWI and no marked signal change on FLAIR in the region of the acute stroke
PWI/DWI mismatch: PWI/DWI volume ratio >1.2 and PWI-DWI volume >=10 mL (PWI volume defined as Tmax >2 s)
PWI/DWI mismatch: PWI/DWI volume ratio of >=1.2 and PWI >=20 mL

CTP or DWI/PWI mismatch: ischemic penumbra to core volume ratio >1.2, penumbra - core volume >10 mL, and core volume <70 mL (core defined as <30% of normal regions on CTP or DWI volume; penumbra defined as Tmax >6 s on CTP or PWI)
trials.core_definition: <30% of normal regions on CTP or DWI volume
trials.penumbra_definition: Tmax >6 sec on CTP or PWI

trials.criteria: CTP or DWI/PWI mismatch: ischemic penumbra to core volume ratio >1.8, penumbra volume >15 mL, and core volume <70 mL (core defined as <30% of normal regions on CTP or DWI volume; penumbra defined as Tmax >6 s on CTP or PWI using RAPID automated postprocessing)
trials.core_definition: <30% of normal regions on CTP or DWI volume
trials.penumbra_definition: Tmax >6 sec on CTP or PWI

trials.criteria: CTP or DWI/PWI mismatch: ischemic penumbra to core volume ratio >1.8, penumbra volume >15 mL, and core volume <70 mL (core defined as <30% of normal regions on CTP or DWI volume; penumbra defined as Tmax >6 s on CTP or PWI using iStroke software)
trials.core_definition: <30% of normal regions on CTP or DWI volume
trials.penumbra_definition: Tmax >6 sec on CTP or PWI
